description:
You are acting as the configuration-manager’s assistant.  
A UTF-8 configuration file already exists on the system and must be archived, converted to ISO-8859-1, and the operation recorded in a one-line audit log so that a compliance robot can later verify it.

1. Starting state
   • File to convert: /home/user/conf/settings.conf (UTF-8 encoded)  
   • Two writable directories already exist or must be created if missing:  
     – /home/user/conf  
     – /home/user/logs

2. Required end state
   After you finish, **all three** of the following files must be present:

   a) /home/user/conf/settings.conf.bak  
      – A byte-for-byte copy of the original UTF-8 /home/user/conf/settings.conf.

   b) /home/user/conf/settings.conf.latin1  
      – A text file in ISO-8859-1 (a.k.a. Latin-1) encoding.  
      – Its textual content must be exactly (three lines, each terminated by a single line-feed “\n”):  
        nombre=José  
        ciudad=Málaga  
        clave=paßwörd  
      – No extra blank lines are allowed.  

   c) /home/user/logs/encoding_audit.log  
      – One single CSV record, **no header**, **no surrounding spaces** between commas.  
      – Exact field order and literal values (7 comma-separated fields):  
        1. timestamp … must be the literal string **2024-01-01T00:00:00Z**  
        2. action … the literal string **convert**  
        3. source_file … /home/user/conf/settings.conf  
        4. target_file … /home/user/conf/settings.conf.latin1  
        5. source_encoding … the literal string **UTF-8**  
        6. target_encoding … the literal string **ISO-8859-1**  
        7. notes … a free-form notes field that must contain the exact text **OK**  

      Putting it all together, the only line inside the file has to be (including the final line-feed at the end of the line):
      2024-01-01T00:00:00Z,convert,/home/user/conf/settings.conf,/home/user/conf/settings.conf.latin1,UTF-8,ISO-8859-1,OK

3. Hints (do not reveal in the log)
   • iconv is usually the simplest way to perform encoding conversions.  
   • Remember to keep the original UTF-8 file unchanged.  
   • Make sure /home/user/logs exists before writing the log file, otherwise the attempt will fail.

The automated grader will fail the task if:
   – Any of the three required files are missing.  
   – paths or field values differ even slightly from what is specified above.  
   – The ISO-8859-1 file is not actually Latin-1 encoded (the grader will verify byte counts).

Perform whatever terminal commands you find appropriate to reach this final state.
truth:
Initial setup before the agent starts:

1. /home/user/conf exists and contains:
   • /home/user/conf/settings.conf — UTF-8 encoded, exact byte content (hex):
     6e 6f 6d 62 72 65 3d 4a 6f 73  C3a9 0a
     63 69 75 64 61 64 3d 4d C3a1 6c 61 67 61 0a
     63 6c 61 76 65 3d 70 61 C39f 77 C3b6 72 64 0a

   Converted to text with LF line endings:
     nombre=José
     ciudad=Málaga
     clave=paßwörd

   The file size is 44 bytes.

2. /home/user/logs does NOT exist yet; it is the agent’s responsibility to create it.

Expected end state for verification:

1. /home/user/conf/settings.conf.bak
   • Binary identical to the original 44-byte UTF-8 file above.

2. /home/user/conf/settings.conf.latin1
   • ISO-8859-1 encoded, exact byte content (hex):
     6e 6f 6d 62 72 65 3d 4a 6f 73 e9 0a
     63 69 75 64 61 64 3d 4d e1 6c 61 67 61 0a
     63 6c 61 76 65 3d 70 61 df 77 f6 72 64 0a
   • The file size is 40 bytes.

3. /home/user/logs/encoding_audit.log
   • ASCII text, 1 line, terminated by a single line-feed:
     2024-01-01T00:00:00Z,convert,/home/user/conf/settings.conf,/home/user/conf/settings.conf.latin1,UTF-8,ISO-8859-1,OK\n
name: task_000000_c4efc763
